text:
Day five of theOlympic Gamesis underway with 17 gold medal events taking place inParis.

Beth Potter got a huge day for Team GB up and running with a bronze medal in the women’s triathlon event.Alex Yee topped that with a sensational late surgein the men’s event, claiming gold after hauling back New Zealand’s Hayden Wilde in the dying embers of the race.

The gold rush continued as Lauren Henry, Hannah Scott, Lola Anderson and Georgie Brayshawstaged a sublime fightback of their ownin the women’s quad sculls final, beating Netherlands in a photo finish.

Elsewhere, Andrea Spendolini-Sirieix –daughter of television presenter Fred Sirieix– and partner Lois Toulson have clinched bronze in the women’s 10-metre synchro final.

Over in the BMX park, Kieran Reilly fell just short of gold but was still delighted to claim a silver medal in the men’s freestyle final.

Away from Team GB, the star-studded USA basketball team which includes Kevin Durant and LeBron James returns to action against South Sudan.

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

Another day at the Paris Olympics is in the books! Here’s what is coming up for you tomorrow…

Thursday, August 1 – Olympic schedule

China’s table tennis star Wang Chuqin has seen his bat accidentally broken by a photographer at the Olympic Games in Paris.

‘I couldn’t understand why the photographers would do that,’ he said. ‘I guess they didn’t mean it. I can’t do anything now that it’s already happened. I believe I’ll still be able to play well with my back-up bat.’

WORLD RECORD! ð¥Pan Zhanle breaks his own WR with a truly stunning swim in the men’s 100m freestyle final.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/4aw2myrD6L

He. Is. Incredible. ð¥No-one in history has completed the 200m butterfly-breaststroke double. Until now.Leon Marchand wins his second gold of the night and third of the games, this time in the men’s 200m breaststroke with an Olympic record.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/7kNaEvuXIn

Leon Marchand wins two gold medals in one night! Incredible from the 22-year-old Frenchman, who has set a new Olympic record of 2:05.85 seconds.

‘Leon Marchand has done the unthinkable, he’s done the double,’ former Olympic swimmer Andy Jameson told the BBC.

‘Double Olympic champion in one session. Not even Michael Phelps tried that. The audacity. 200m butterfly and 200m breaststroke, I cannot believe he even tried it, never mind win them both.’

Olympic tennis starsIga Swiatekand Danielle Collins had a nasty fall-out on Wednesday, with the Polish world No.1 branded ‘insincere’ by her opponent.

Click here for the full story.

An Olympic venue has been evacuated over a weather warning in Paris, with fans advised to go home…

Click here for the full story.

Incredible!@TeamGB’s Megs Havers – the youngest archer at Paris 2024 – delivered the ultimate shoot-off shot to stun France’s Amelie Cordeau! ð²The 16-year-old moves on to the last eight. ð#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/WCNTiM4sYW

ð¥ð¥ð¥ð¥ð¥ð¥ð¥ð¥Katie Ledecky wins her EIGHTH Olympic gold medal.The ð of women’s swimming adds to her collection, winning the 1500m freestyle and breaking her own Olympic record in the process.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/ezIt0pGyyf

Katie Ledecky has secured gold for Team USA in the women’s 1500m freestyle final. That was knackering just to watch, so can only imagine how she feels!

She finished half-a-pool length clear of France’s Anastasiya Kirpichnikova, who took silver, while the bronze went to Germany’s Isabel Gose.

Andy Murray is hoping to land a medal in the men’s doubles – and after the shock exit of Spain’s superteam – his chances have arguably gone up.

Murray and Dan Evans will play their men’s doubles quarter-final matchtomorrowas they look to book a spot in the last four at Roland-Garros.

The British pair face Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul in the last eight after the Americans dumped out Dutch duo Robin Haase and Jean-Julien Rojer.

Murray/ Evans vs Fritz/Paul is the fourth scheduled match on Court Suzanne Lenglen, with the first match of the day getting underway at 11am. It is hard to give an exact time – but the British duo should get started around6.30pm.

This is Murray’s final tournament before he officially enters retirement.

Andy Murray is hoping to land a medal in the men’s doubles – and after the shock exit of Spain’s dream team – his chances have arguably gone up.

Murray and Dan Evans will play their men’s doubles quarter-final matchtomorrowas they look to book a spot in the last four at Roland-Garros.

The British pair face Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul in the last eight after the Americans dumped out Dutch duo Robin Haase and Jean-Julien Rojer.

Murray/ Evans vs Fritz/Paul is the fourth scheduled match on Court Suzanne Lenglen, with the first match of the day getting underway at 11am. It is hard to give an exact time – but the British duo should get started around6.30pm.

This is Murray’s final tournament before he officially enters retirement.

LEWIS RICHARDSON! ð¥ð¬ð§@TeamGB’s boxers have their first victory of the Paris 2024 Olympics Games, thanks to the man from Colchester. ð#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/Z6qHHDaY58

Rafael Nadal and Carlos Alcaraz’ Olympics doubles run has come to a dramatic end! They’ve just lost to Team USA.

The Spanish dream team suffered a shock defeat to Rajeev Ram and Austin Krajicek at Roland-Garros and are now fully out of medal contention.

Nadal, almost certainly to be playing his last Olympics at the age of 38,crashed out in singles to Novak Djokovicearlier in the tournament.

Krajicek and Ram dash Alcaraz and Nadal’s golden dreams!#Tennispic.twitter.com/VOaqba7bc1

British tennis star Liam Broady has called Andy Murray a ‘cry baby’ after the 37-year-old broke down in tears at the Paris Olympics on Tuesday.

Click here for the full story.

Japan’s Shinnosuke Oka is the men’s all-around Olympic champion! ð¯ðµJoe Fraser finishes 5th and Jake Jarman in 7th. ð¬ð§#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/2l3nsEOt3j

A high-class and eventful men’s all-round final ends with Japan’s Shinnosuke Oka taking gold.

China’s Boheng Zang finished 0.233 points behind Oka in the silver medal position, while Xiao Ruoteng took bronze.

Great Britain’s Joe Fraser and Jake Jarman finished fifth and seventh respectively.

‘It is amazing to see Japan with the domination, beautiful gymnastics – they have been so consistent,’ former Olympian Louis Smith said.

‘Team GB can hold their head high.’

He might be out of medal contention but Jake Jarman is still in good spirits ðð¤£#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/IKgxwNVrBg

Canadian gymnast Felix Dolci suffered a scary moment at the Olympics after an ‘equipment malfunction’ sent him flying off the horizontal bar and crashing into the floor.

Click here for the full story.

There’s also some Olympic football on today.

Brazil are playing Spain in Bordeaux but thematch did not go the way women’s football legend Marta would have wanted.

We’ll have more swimming action to bring you later on but have you ever wondered why swimmers often slap their legs before races? Wellhere’s the answer!

Andy Murray and Dan Evans’ opponents for the men’s doubles quarter-finals has just been confirmed.

The Team GB pair will face Team USA’s Taylor Fritz and Tommy Paul, who just eliminated the Netherlands’ Robin Haase and Jean-Julien Rojer in their second-round match at Roland-Garros.

If they beat the Americans, Murray and Evans will be guaranteed a spot in a Paris medal match.

Here’s how they became genuine contenders in the French capital.

We all sawTeam USA and Simone Biles light up the women’s team gymnastics final yesterdayand now it’s the men’s turn to show of their skills.

This isn’t actually a team event but Great Britain have two competitors in the individual final – Jake Jarman and Joe Fraser.

The event sees each athlete have a go on six different apparatus – floor, rings, pommel horse, parallel bars, horizontal bar and vault.

Hannah Scott helped Team GB’s rowers win gold earlier today and just look what it meant to some of her friends back home…

Coleraine have cheered as local Team GB hero Hannah Scott who is a member of Bann Rowing Club won gold in the Paris#OlympicsScott won the won the gold in the women’s quadruple scullspic.twitter.com/F8wtIAsUXH

ArrowMORE:Olympic venue evacuated after severe weather warning in Paris

ArrowMORE:British star calls Andy Murray a ‘cry baby’ for what he did at Paris Olympics

title: Olympics schedule and results: Triathlon, rowing, BMX and gymnastics | Metro News
description: Latest news and live updates from 2024 Paris Olympic Games on day 5 (July 31) schedule: triathlon, rowing, gymnastics, BMX and basketball.
